description_long: This church is a stone masonry structure with a prominent central nave and a steeply pitched slate roof. The building features a tall, pointed steeple rising from a square bell tower, which is integrated into the main body of the church. The exterior walls are constructed from light brown stone blocks, giving the church a weathered, historic appearance. Several arched windows are visible, including a large stained glass window on the front facade, and smaller windows along the sides. The main entrance is a wooden door set into a simple, gabled porch. Architectural details such as buttresses and varying roof heights add depth and complexity to the structure.
description: A historic stone church with a tall steeple, slate roof, and stained glass windows, featuring a wooden entrance and detailed masonry.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long nave, stone walls, and the base of the steeple with multiple roof levels.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance, large stained glass window, and the full height of the steeple.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the stonework, side windows, and the rear extension of the church.
description_view_3: Rear view with a smaller wooden door, simple gable, and the steeple rising above the roofline.
